_readme: This file is a handoff document for the backend developer.
The frontend (fitClub) reads a /sider-menu API response that has two arrays: `menu` and `permissions`.
The `menu` array controls which sidebar items are VISIBLE — DO NOT add new entries to this array.
The `permissions` array controls which action BUTTONS are visible inside pages (Edit, Delete, Mark Present, Download, etc.).
When a role is saved with a permission key, the backend must include that permission object in the `permissions` array of the sider-menu response.
The frontend checks: permissions.some(p => p.permission === 'PERMISSION_NAME') to show or hide specific action buttons.

howVisibilityWorks.menu: Controls which SIDEBAR items are visible. Backend must filter this array by role — only include menu items the role has access to. If User Management is not in this array, it will NOT appear in the sidebar at all.
howVisibilityWorks.permissions: Controls which ACTION BUTTONS are visible inside pages (Edit, Delete, Mark Present, Download, etc.). Backend must include only the action-level permission objects the role is allowed to perform.
howVisibilityWorks.example: Role has no User Management access → backend omits key '9' from `menu` → sidebar hides User Management entirely. Role has User Management but no delete → backend includes key '9' in `menu` but omits '9-1-delete' from `permissions` → sidebar shows the menu but Delete button is hidden inside the page.

menu._comment: Only include items this role has access to. Missing items = hidden from sidebar.
menu.label: User Management

permissions._comment: Only include action-level permissions this role is allowed to perform.
permissions.label: All Users

newPermissionsRequired._note: All items below must be returned in the `permissions` array when the role has that permission granted. The `menu` array does NOT change.

keyTakeaways: The `menu` array in the API response does NOT need any new entries.
Only add new objects to the `permissions` array based on what the role has access to.
Each permission object must have: key (string), label (string), permission (string).
The frontend uses BOTH `p.key` and `p.permission` checks — send both fields.
If a role does not have a permission, simply omit that object from the permissions array.

exampleFullResponse._description: Example: A role that can view All Employees, edit employees, but NOT delete them.
